Lucas Waldburger

PhD Student
UC Berkeley
lwaldburger (at) berkeley.edu


About Me

I am a PhD Student in Bioengineering at UC Berkeley advised by Jay Keasling and Patrick Shih. My research focuses on developing molecular technologies and algorithms to model the evolutionary dynamics of gene regulatory systems.

Previously, I studied Electrical Engineering & Computer Science, where I worked on multi-agent systems, and Molecular & Cell Biology, where I worked on biological feedback control.

I am grateful to have my work supported by the NSF Graduate Research Fellowship.

Research Interests

  • Machine learning for biological design: active learning and model-guided design of proteins and gene-regulatory elements.
  • Evolutionary modeling of gene regulation: learning the dynamics that shape transcriptional control across evolutionary space.
  • High-throughput & self-driving labs: closing the loop between large-scale experiments and predictive models.

    Current Opinion in Biotechnology, 2023. Self-Driving Labs

    mSystems, 2023. Computational BiologyEvolution

    Nature Biotechnology, 2025. Synthetic BiologyDirected Evolution
